fields.name: secretmanager_projects_locations_get
fields.description: Gets information about a location.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_locations_list
fields.description: Lists information about the supported locations for this service. This method can be called in two ways: * **List all public locations:** Use the path `GET /v1/locations`. * **List project-visible locations:** Use the path `GET /v1/projects/{project_id}/locations`. This may include public locations as well as private or other locations specifically visible to the project.

args.name: pageSize
args.description: The maximum number of results to return. If not set, the service selects a default.

args.name: pageToken
args.description: A page token received from the `next_page_token` field in the response. Send that page token to receive the subsequent page.

args.name: extraLocationTypes
args.description: Optional. Do not use this field. It is unsupported and is ignored unless explicitly documented otherwise. This is primarily for internal usage.

args.name: filter
args.description: A filter to narrow down results to a preferred subset. The filtering language accepts strings like `"displayName=tokyo"`, and is documented in more detail in [AIP-160](https://google.aip.dev/160).

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_locations_secrets_get
fields.description: Gets metadata for a given Secret.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_locations_secrets_getIamPolicy
fields.description: Gets the access control policy for a secret. Returns empty policy if the secret exists and does not have a policy set.

args.name: options_requestedPolicyVersion
args.description: Optional. The maximum policy version that will be used to format the policy. Valid values are 0, 1, and 3. Requests specifying an invalid value will be rejected. Requests for policies with any conditional role bindings must specify version 3. Policies with no conditional role bindings may specify any valid value or leave the field unset. The policy in the response might use the policy version that you specified, or it might use a lower policy version. For example, if you specify version 3, but the policy has no conditional role bindings, the response uses version 1. To learn which resources support conditions in their IAM policies, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/help/conditions/resource-policies).

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_locations_secrets_list
fields.description: Lists Secrets.

args.name: pageToken
args.description: Optional. Pagination token, returned earlier via ListSecretsResponse.next_page_token.

args.name: pageSize
args.description: Optional. The maximum number of results to be returned in a single page. If set to 0, the server decides the number of results to return. If the number is greater than 25000, it is capped at 25000.

args.name: filter
args.description: Optional. Filter string, adhering to the rules in [List-operation filtering](https://cloud.google.com/secret-manager/docs/filtering). List only secrets matching the filter. If filter is empty, all secrets are listed.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_locations_secrets_versions_list
fields.description: Lists SecretVersions. This call does not return secret data.

args.name: filter
args.description: Optional. Filter string, adhering to the rules in [List-operation filtering](https://cloud.google.com/secret-manager/docs/filtering). List only secret versions matching the filter. If filter is empty, all secret versions are listed.

args.name: pageSize
args.description: Optional. The maximum number of results to be returned in a single page. If set to 0, the server decides the number of results to return. If the number is greater than 25000, it is capped at 25000.

args.name: pageToken
args.description: Optional. Pagination token, returned earlier via ListSecretVersionsResponse.next_page_token][].

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_locations_secrets_versions_get
fields.description: Gets metadata for a SecretVersion. `projects/*/secrets/*/versions/latest` is an alias to the most recently created SecretVersion.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_locations_secrets_versions_access
fields.description: Accesses a SecretVersion. This call returns the secret data. `projects/*/secrets/*/versions/latest` is an alias to the most recently created SecretVersion.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_secrets_get
fields.description: Gets metadata for a given Secret.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_secrets_list
fields.description: Lists Secrets.

args.name: pageToken
args.description: Optional. Pagination token, returned earlier via ListSecretsResponse.next_page_token.

args.name: pageSize
args.description: Optional. The maximum number of results to be returned in a single page. If set to 0, the server decides the number of results to return. If the number is greater than 25000, it is capped at 25000.

args.name: filter
args.description: Optional. Filter string, adhering to the rules in [List-operation filtering](https://cloud.google.com/secret-manager/docs/filtering). List only secrets matching the filter. If filter is empty, all secrets are listed.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_secrets_getIamPolicy
fields.description: Gets the access control policy for a secret. Returns empty policy if the secret exists and does not have a policy set.

args.name: options_requestedPolicyVersion
args.description: Optional. The maximum policy version that will be used to format the policy. Valid values are 0, 1, and 3. Requests specifying an invalid value will be rejected. Requests for policies with any conditional role bindings must specify version 3. Policies with no conditional role bindings may specify any valid value or leave the field unset. The policy in the response might use the policy version that you specified, or it might use a lower policy version. For example, if you specify version 3, but the policy has no conditional role bindings, the response uses version 1. To learn which resources support conditions in their IAM policies, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/help/conditions/resource-policies).

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_secrets_versions_list
fields.description: Lists SecretVersions. This call does not return secret data.

args.name: pageToken
args.description: Optional. Pagination token, returned earlier via ListSecretVersionsResponse.next_page_token][].

args.name: filter
args.description: Optional. Filter string, adhering to the rules in [List-operation filtering](https://cloud.google.com/secret-manager/docs/filtering). List only secret versions matching the filter. If filter is empty, all secret versions are listed.

args.name: pageSize
args.description: Optional. The maximum number of results to be returned in a single page. If set to 0, the server decides the number of results to return. If the number is greater than 25000, it is capped at 25000.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_secrets_versions_access
fields.description: Accesses a SecretVersion. This call returns the secret data. `projects/*/secrets/*/versions/latest` is an alias to the most recently created SecretVersion.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_secrets_versions_get
fields.description: Gets metadata for a SecretVersion. `projects/*/secrets/*/versions/latest` is an alias to the most recently created SecretVersion.

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: Location
types.description: A resource that represents a Google Cloud location.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: Cross-service attributes for the location. For example {"cloud.googleapis.com/region": "us-east1"}

fields.name: locationId
fields.description: The canonical id for this location. For example: `"us-east1"`.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Resource name for the location, which may vary between implementations. For example: `"projects/example-project/locations/us-east1"`

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: The friendly name for this location, typically a nearby city name. For example, "Tokyo".

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Service-specific metadata. For example the available capacity at the given location.

types.name: JSON
types.description: The `JSON` scalar type represents JSON values as specified by [ECMA-404](http://www.ecma-international.org/publications/files/ECMA-ST/ECMA-404.pdf).

types.name: ListLocationsResponse
types.description: The response message for Locations.ListLocations.

fields.name: locations
fields.description: A list of locations that matches the specified filter in the request.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: The standard List next-page token.

types.name: Secret
types.description: A Secret is a logical secret whose value and versions can be accessed. A Secret is made up of zero or more SecretVersions that represent the secret data.

fields.name: topics
fields.description: Optional. A list of up to 10 Pub/Sub topics to which messages are published when control plane operations are called on the secret or its versions.

fields.name: expireTime
fields.description: Optional. Timestamp in UTC when the Secret is scheduled to expire. This is always provided on output, regardless of what was sent on input.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Output only. The resource name of the Secret in the format `projects/*/secrets/*`.

fields.name: ttl
fields.description: Input only. The TTL for the Secret.

fields.name: tags
fields.description: Optional. Input only. Immutable. Mapping of Tag keys/values directly bound to this resource. For example: "123/environment": "production", "123/costCenter": "marketing" Tags are used to organize and group resources. Tags can be used to control policy evaluation for the resource.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: Optional. Custom metadata about the secret. Annotations are distinct from various forms of labels. Annotations exist to allow client tools to store their own state information without requiring a database. Annotation keys must be between 1 and 63 characters long, have a UTF-8 encoding of maximum 128 bytes, begin and end with an alphanumeric character ([a-z0-9A-Z]), and may have dashes (-), underscores (_), dots (.), and alphanumerics in between these symbols. The total size of annotation keys and values must be less than 16KiB.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: The labels assigned to this Secret. Label keys must be between 1 and 63 characters long, have a UTF-8 encoding of maximum 128 bytes, and must conform to the following PCRE regular expression: `\p{Ll}\p{Lo}{0,62}` Label values must be between 0 and 63 characters long, have a UTF-8 encoding of maximum 128 bytes, and must conform to the following PCRE regular expression: `[\p{Ll}\p{Lo}\p{N}_-]{0,63}` No more than 64 labels can be assigned to a given resource.

fields.name: etag
fields.description: Optional. Etag of the currently stored Secret.

fields.name: versionAliases
fields.description: Optional. Mapping from version alias to version name. A version alias is a string with a maximum length of 63 characters and can contain uppercase and lowercase letters, numerals, and the hyphen (`-`) and underscore ('_') characters. An alias string must start with a letter and cannot be the string 'latest' or 'NEW'. No more than 50 aliases can be assigned to a given secret. Version-Alias pairs will be viewable via GetSecret and modifiable via UpdateSecret. Access by alias is only be supported on GetSecretVersion and AccessSecretVersion.

fields.name: versionDestroyTtl
fields.description: Optional. Secret Version TTL after destruction request This is a part of the Delayed secret version destroy feature. For secret with TTL>0, version destruction doesn't happen immediately on calling destroy instead the version goes to a disabled state and destruction happens after the TTL expires.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. The time at which the Secret was created.

types.name: Topic
types.description: A Pub/Sub topic which Secret Manager will publish to when control plane events occur on this secret.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Identifier. The resource name of the Pub/Sub topic that will be published to, in the following format: `projects/*/topics/*`. For publication to succeed, the Secret Manager service agent must have the `pubsub.topic.publish` permission on the topic. The Pub/Sub Publisher role (`roles/pubsub.publisher`) includes this permission.

types.name: Rotation
types.description: The rotation time and period for a Secret. At next_rotation_time, Secret Manager will send a Pub/Sub notification to the topics configured on the Secret. Secret.topics must be set to configure rotation.

fields.name: nextRotationTime
fields.description: Optional. Timestamp in UTC at which the Secret is scheduled to rotate. Cannot be set to less than 300s (5 min) in the future and at most 3153600000s (100 years). next_rotation_time MUST be set if rotation_period is set.

fields.name: rotationPeriod
fields.description: Input only. The Duration between rotation notifications. Must be in seconds and at least 3600s (1h) and at most 3153600000s (100 years). If rotation_period is set, next_rotation_time must be set. next_rotation_time will be advanced by this period when the service automatically sends rotation notifications.

types.name: CustomerManagedEncryption
types.description: Configuration for encrypting secret payloads using customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK).

fields.name: kmsKeyName
fields.description: Required. The resource name of the Cloud KMS CryptoKey used to encrypt secret payloads. For secrets using the UserManaged replication policy type, Cloud KMS CryptoKeys must reside in the same location as the replica location. For secrets using the Automatic replication policy type, Cloud KMS CryptoKeys must reside in `global`. The expected format is `projects/*/locations/*/keyRings/*/cryptoKeys/*`.

types.name: Replication
types.description: A policy that defines the replication and encryption configuration of data.

types.name: UserManaged
types.description: A replication policy that replicates the Secret payload into the locations specified in Replication.UserManaged.replicas

fields.name: replicas
fields.description: Required. The list of Replicas for this Secret. Cannot be empty.

types.name: Replica
types.description: Represents a Replica for this Secret.

fields.name: location
fields.description: The canonical IDs of the location to replicate data. For example: `"us-east1"`.

types.name: Automatic
types.description: A replication policy that replicates the Secret payload without any restrictions.

types.name: Policy
types.description: An Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy, which specifies access controls for Google Cloud resources. A `Policy` is a collection of `bindings`. A `binding` binds one or more `members`, or principals, to a single `role`. Principals can be user accounts, service accounts, Google groups, and domains (such as G Suite). A `role` is a named list of permissions; each `role` can be an IAM predefined role or a user-created custom role. For some types of Google Cloud resources, a `binding` can also specify a `condition`, which is a logical expression that allows access to a resource only if the expression evaluates to `true`. A condition can add constraints based on attributes of the request, the resource, or both. To learn which resources support conditions in their IAM policies, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/help/conditions/resource-policies). **JSON example:** ``` { "bindings": [ { "role": "roles/resourcemanager.organizationAdmin", "members": [ "user:mike@example.com", "group:admins@example.com", "domain:google.com", "serviceAccount:my-project-id@appspot.gserviceaccount.com" ] }, { "role": "roles/resourcemanager.organizationViewer", "members": [ "user:eve@example.com" ], "condition": { "title": "expirable access", "description": "Does not grant access after Sep 2020", "expression": "request.time < timestamp('2020-10-01T00:00:00.000Z')", } } ], "etag": "BwWWja0YfJA=", "version": 3 } ``` **YAML example:** ``` bindings: - members: - user:mike@example.com - group:admins@example.com - domain:google.com - serviceAccount:my-project-id@appspot.gserviceaccount.com role: roles/resourcemanager.organizationAdmin - members: - user:eve@example.com role: roles/resourcemanager.organizationViewer condition: title: expirable access description: Does not grant access after Sep 2020 expression: request.time < timestamp('2020-10-01T00:00:00.000Z') etag: BwWWja0YfJA= version: 3 ``` For a description of IAM and its features, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/).

fields.name: bindings
fields.description: Associates a list of `members`, or principals, with a `role`. Optionally, may specify a `condition` that determines how and when the `bindings` are applied. Each of the `bindings` must contain at least one principal. The `bindings` in a `Policy` can refer to up to 1,500 principals; up to 250 of these principals can be Google groups. Each occurrence of a principal counts towards these limits. For example, if the `bindings` grant 50 different roles to `user:alice@example.com`, and not to any other principal, then you can add another 1,450 principals to the `bindings` in the `Policy`.

fields.name: auditConfigs
fields.description: Specifies cloud audit logging configuration for this policy.

fields.name: version
fields.description: Specifies the format of the policy. Valid values are `0`, `1`, and `3`. Requests that specify an invalid value are rejected. Any operation that affects conditional role bindings must specify version `3`. This requirement applies to the following operations: * Getting a policy that includes a conditional role binding * Adding a conditional role binding to a policy * Changing a conditional role binding in a policy * Removing any role binding, with or without a condition, from a policy that includes conditions **Important:** If you use IAM Conditions, you must include the `etag` field whenever you call `setIamPolicy`. If you omit this field, then IAM allows you to overwrite a version `3` policy with a version `1` policy, and all of the conditions in the version `3` policy are lost. If a policy does not include any conditions, operations on that policy may specify any valid version or leave the field unset. To learn which resources support conditions in their IAM policies, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/help/conditions/resource-policies).

fields.name: etag
fields.description: `etag` is used for optimistic concurrency control as a way to help prevent simultaneous updates of a policy from overwriting each other. It is strongly suggested that systems make use of the `etag` in the read-modify-write cycle to perform policy updates in order to avoid race conditions: An `etag` is returned in the response to `getIamPolicy`, and systems are expected to put that etag in the request to `setIamPolicy` to ensure that their change will be applied to the same version of the policy. **Important:** If you use IAM Conditions, you must include the `etag` field whenever you call `setIamPolicy`. If you omit this field, then IAM allows you to overwrite a version `3` policy with a version `1` policy, and all of the conditions in the version `3` policy are lost.

types.name: Binding
types.description: Associates `members`, or principals, with a `role`.

fields.name: members
fields.description: Specifies the principals requesting access for a Google Cloud resource. `members` can have the following values: * `allUsers`: A special identifier that represents anyone who is on the internet; with or without a Google account. * `allAuthenticatedUsers`: A special identifier that represents anyone who is authenticated with a Google account or a service account. Does not include identities that come from external identity providers (IdPs) through identity federation. * `user:{emailid}`: An email address that represents a specific Google account. For example, `alice@example.com` . * `serviceAccount:{emailid}`: An email address that represents a Google service account. For example, `my-other-app@appspot.gserviceaccount.com`. * `serviceAccount:{projectid}.svc.id.goog[{namespace}/{kubernetes-sa}]`: An identifier for a [Kubernetes service account](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/kubernetes-service-accounts). For example, `my-project.svc.id.goog[my-namespace/my-kubernetes-sa]`. * `group:{emailid}`: An email address that represents a Google group. For example, `admins@example.com`. * `domain:{domain}`: The G Suite domain (primary) that represents all the users of that domain. For example, `google.com` or `example.com`. * `principal://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/subject/{subject_attribute_value}`: A single identity in a workforce identity pool. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/group/{group_id}`: All workforce identities in a group. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/attribute.{attribute_name}/{attribute_value}`: All workforce identities with a specific attribute value. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/*`: All identities in a workforce identity pool. * `principal://iam.googleapis.com/projects/{project_number}/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/{pool_id}/subject/{subject_attribute_value}`: A single identity in a workload identity pool. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/projects/{project_number}/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/{pool_id}/group/{group_id}`: A workload identity pool group. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/projects/{project_number}/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/{pool_id}/attribute.{attribute_name}/{attribute_value}`: All identities in a workload identity pool with a certain attribute. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/projects/{project_number}/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/{pool_id}/*`: All identities in a workload identity pool. * `deleted:user:{emailid}?uid={uniqueid}`: An email address (plus unique identifier) representing a user that has been recently deleted. For example, `alice@example.com?uid=123456789012345678901`. If the user is recovered, this value reverts to `user:{emailid}` and the recovered user retains the role in the binding. * `deleted:serviceAccount:{emailid}?uid={uniqueid}`: An email address (plus unique identifier) representing a service account that has been recently deleted. For example, `my-other-app@appspot.gserviceaccount.com?uid=123456789012345678901`. If the service account is undeleted, this value reverts to `serviceAccount:{emailid}` and the undeleted service account retains the role in the binding. * `deleted:group:{emailid}?uid={uniqueid}`: An email address (plus unique identifier) representing a Google group that has been recently deleted. For example, `admins@example.com?uid=123456789012345678901`. If the group is recovered, this value reverts to `group:{emailid}` and the recovered group retains the role in the binding. * `deleted:principal://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/subject/{subject_attribute_value}`: Deleted single identity in a workforce identity pool. For example, `deleted:principal://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/my-pool-id/subject/my-subject-attribute-value`.

fields.name: role
fields.description: Role that is assigned to the list of `members`, or principals. For example, `roles/viewer`, `roles/editor`, or `roles/owner`. For an overview of the IAM roles and permissions, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/roles-overview). For a list of the available pre-defined roles, see [here](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/understanding-roles).

types.name: Expr
types.description: Represents a textual expression in the Common Expression Language (CEL) syntax. CEL is a C-like expression language. The syntax and semantics of CEL are documented at https://github.com/google/cel-spec. Example (Comparison): title: "Summary size limit" description: "Determines if a summary is less than 100 chars" expression: "document.summary.size() < 100" Example (Equality): title: "Requestor is owner" description: "Determines if requestor is the document owner" expression: "document.owner == request.auth.claims.email" Example (Logic): title: "Public documents" description: "Determine whether the document should be publicly visible" expression: "document.type != 'private' && document.type != 'internal'" Example (Data Manipulation): title: "Notification string" description: "Create a notification string with a timestamp." expression: "'New message received at ' + string(document.create_time)" The exact variables and functions that may be referenced within an expression are determined by the service that evaluates it. See the service documentation for additional information.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional. Description of the expression. This is a longer text which describes the expression, e.g. when hovered over it in a UI.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Optional. Title for the expression, i.e. a short string describing its purpose. This can be used e.g. in UIs which allow to enter the expression.

fields.name: location
fields.description: Optional. String indicating the location of the expression for error reporting, e.g. a file name and a position in the file.

fields.name: expression
fields.description: Textual representation of an expression in Common Expression Language syntax.

types.name: AuditConfig
types.description: Specifies the audit configuration for a service. The configuration determines which permission types are logged, and what identities, if any, are exempted from logging. An AuditConfig must have one or more AuditLogConfigs. If there are AuditConfigs for both `allServices` and a specific service, the union of the two AuditConfigs is used for that service: the log_types specified in each AuditConfig are enabled, and the exempted_members in each AuditLogConfig are exempted. Example Policy with multiple AuditConfigs: { "audit_configs": [ { "service": "allServices", "audit_log_configs": [ { "log_type": "DATA_READ", "exempted_members": [ "user:jose@example.com" ] }, { "log_type": "DATA_WRITE" }, { "log_type": "ADMIN_READ" } ] }, { "service": "sampleservice.googleapis.com", "audit_log_configs": [ { "log_type": "DATA_READ" }, { "log_type": "DATA_WRITE", "exempted_members": [ "user:aliya@example.com" ] } ] } ] } For sampleservice, this policy enables DATA_READ, DATA_WRITE and ADMIN_READ logging. It also exempts `jose@example.com` from DATA_READ logging, and `aliya@example.com` from DATA_WRITE logging.

fields.name: auditLogConfigs
fields.description: The configuration for logging of each type of permission.

fields.name: service
fields.description: Specifies a service that will be enabled for audit logging. For example, `storage.googleapis.com`, `cloudsql.googleapis.com`. `allServices` is a special value that covers all services.

types.name: AuditLogConfig
types.description: Provides the configuration for logging a type of permissions. Example: { "audit_log_configs": [ { "log_type": "DATA_READ", "exempted_members": [ "user:jose@example.com" ] }, { "log_type": "DATA_WRITE" } ] } This enables 'DATA_READ' and 'DATA_WRITE' logging, while exempting jose@example.com from DATA_READ logging.

fields.name: exemptedMembers
fields.description: Specifies the identities that do not cause logging for this type of permission. Follows the same format of Binding.members.

types.name: query_secretmanager_projects_locations_secrets_getIamPolicy_auditConfigs_items_auditLogConfigs_items_logType
types.description: The log type that this config enables.

types.name: Byte
types.description: The `Byte` scalar type represents byte value as a Buffer

types.name: ListSecretsResponse
types.description: Response message for SecretManagerService.ListSecrets.

fields.name: totalSize
fields.description: The total number of Secrets but 0 when the ListSecretsRequest.filter field is set.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: A token to retrieve the next page of results. Pass this value in ListSecretsRequest.page_token to retrieve the next page.

fields.name: secrets
fields.description: The list of Secrets sorted in reverse by create_time (newest first).

types.name: ListSecretVersionsResponse
types.description: Response message for SecretManagerService.ListSecretVersions.

fields.name: versions
fields.description: The list of SecretVersions sorted in reverse by create_time (newest first).

fields.name: totalSize
fields.description: The total number of SecretVersions but 0 when the ListSecretsRequest.filter field is set.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: A token to retrieve the next page of results. Pass this value in ListSecretVersionsRequest.page_token to retrieve the next page.

types.name: SecretVersion
types.description: A secret version resource in the Secret Manager API.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: Output only. The time at which the SecretVersion was created.

fields.name: scheduledDestroyTime
fields.description: Optional. Output only. Scheduled destroy time for secret version. This is a part of the Delayed secret version destroy feature. For a Secret with a valid version destroy TTL, when a secert version is destroyed, version is moved to disabled state and it is scheduled for destruction Version is destroyed only after the scheduled_destroy_time.

fields.name: clientSpecifiedPayloadChecksum
fields.description: Output only. True if payload checksum specified in SecretPayload object has been received by SecretManagerService on SecretManagerService.AddSecretVersion.

fields.name: destroyTime
fields.description: Output only. The time this SecretVersion was destroyed. Only present if state is DESTROYED.

fields.name: etag
fields.description: Output only. Etag of the currently stored SecretVersion.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Output only. The resource name of the SecretVersion in the format `projects/*/secrets/*/versions/*`. SecretVersion IDs in a Secret start at 1 and are incremented for each subsequent version of the secret.

types.name: query_secretmanager_projects_locations_secrets_versions_list_versions_items_state
types.description: Output only. The current state of the SecretVersion.

types.name: ReplicationStatus
types.description: The replication status of a SecretVersion.

types.name: AutomaticStatus
types.description: The replication status of a SecretVersion using automatic replication. Only populated if the parent Secret has an automatic replication policy.

types.name: CustomerManagedEncryptionStatus
types.description: Describes the status of customer-managed encryption.

fields.name: kmsKeyVersionName
fields.description: Required. The resource name of the Cloud KMS CryptoKeyVersion used to encrypt the secret payload, in the following format: `projects/*/locations/*/keyRings/*/cryptoKeys/*/versions/*`.

types.name: UserManagedStatus
types.description: The replication status of a SecretVersion using user-managed replication. Only populated if the parent Secret has a user-managed replication policy.

fields.name: replicas
fields.description: Output only. The list of replica statuses for the SecretVersion.

types.name: ReplicaStatus
types.description: Describes the status of a user-managed replica for the SecretVersion.

fields.name: location
fields.description: Output only. The canonical ID of the replica location. For example: `"us-east1"`.

types.name: AccessSecretVersionResponse
types.description: Response message for SecretManagerService.AccessSecretVersion.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The resource name of the SecretVersion in the format `projects/*/secrets/*/versions/*` or `projects/*/locations/*/secrets/*/versions/*`.

types.name: SecretPayload
types.description: A secret payload resource in the Secret Manager API. This contains the sensitive secret payload that is associated with a SecretVersion.

fields.name: data
fields.description: The secret data. Must be no larger than 64KiB.

fields.name: dataCrc32c
fields.description: Optional. If specified, SecretManagerService will verify the integrity of the received data on SecretManagerService.AddSecretVersion calls using the crc32c checksum and store it to include in future SecretManagerService.AccessSecretVersion responses. If a checksum is not provided in the SecretManagerService.AddSecretVersion request, the SecretManagerService will generate and store one for you. The CRC32C value is encoded as a Int64 for compatibility, and can be safely downconverted to uint32 in languages that support this type. https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/design_patterns#integer_types

types.name: BigInt
types.description: The `BigInt` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_locations_secrets_addVersion
fields.description: Creates a new SecretVersion containing secret data and attaches it to an existing Secret.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_locations_secrets_delete
fields.description: Deletes a Secret.

args.name: etag
args.description: Optional. Etag of the Secret. The request succeeds if it matches the etag of the currently stored secret object. If the etag is omitted, the request succeeds.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_locations_secrets_patch
fields.description: Updates metadata of an existing Secret.

args.name: updateMask
args.description: Required. Specifies the fields to be updated.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_locations_secrets_create
fields.description: Creates a new Secret containing no SecretVersions.

args.name: secretId
args.description: Required. This must be unique within the project. A secret ID is a string with a maximum length of 255 characters and can contain uppercase and lowercase letters, numerals, and the hyphen (`-`) and underscore (`_`) characters.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_locations_secrets_testIamPermissions
fields.description: Returns permissions that a caller has for the specified secret. If the secret does not exist, this call returns an empty set of permissions, not a NOT_FOUND error. Note: This operation is designed to be used for building permission-aware UIs and command-line tools, not for authorization checking. This operation may "fail open" without warning.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_locations_secrets_setIamPolicy
fields.description: Sets the access control policy on the specified secret. Replaces any existing policy. Permissions on SecretVersions are enforced according to the policy set on the associated Secret.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_locations_secrets_versions_enable
fields.description: Enables a SecretVersion. Sets the state of the SecretVersion to ENABLED.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_locations_secrets_versions_destroy
fields.description: Destroys a SecretVersion. Sets the state of the SecretVersion to DESTROYED and irrevocably destroys the secret data.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_locations_secrets_versions_disable
fields.description: Disables a SecretVersion. Sets the state of the SecretVersion to DISABLED.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_secrets_delete
fields.description: Deletes a Secret.

args.name: etag
args.description: Optional. Etag of the Secret. The request succeeds if it matches the etag of the currently stored secret object. If the etag is omitted, the request succeeds.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_secrets_patch
fields.description: Updates metadata of an existing Secret.

args.name: updateMask
args.description: Required. Specifies the fields to be updated.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_secrets_create
fields.description: Creates a new Secret containing no SecretVersions.

args.name: secretId
args.description: Required. This must be unique within the project. A secret ID is a string with a maximum length of 255 characters and can contain uppercase and lowercase letters, numerals, and the hyphen (`-`) and underscore (`_`) characters.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_secrets_addVersion
fields.description: Creates a new SecretVersion containing secret data and attaches it to an existing Secret.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_secrets_setIamPolicy
fields.description: Sets the access control policy on the specified secret. Replaces any existing policy. Permissions on SecretVersions are enforced according to the policy set on the associated Secret.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_secrets_testIamPermissions
fields.description: Returns permissions that a caller has for the specified secret. If the secret does not exist, this call returns an empty set of permissions, not a NOT_FOUND error. Note: This operation is designed to be used for building permission-aware UIs and command-line tools, not for authorization checking. This operation may "fail open" without warning.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_secrets_versions_destroy
fields.description: Destroys a SecretVersion. Sets the state of the SecretVersion to DESTROYED and irrevocably destroys the secret data.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_secrets_versions_disable
fields.description: Disables a SecretVersion. Sets the state of the SecretVersion to DISABLED.

fields.name: secretmanager_projects_secrets_versions_enable
fields.description: Enables a SecretVersion. Sets the state of the SecretVersion to ENABLED.

types.name: AddSecretVersionRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for SecretManagerService.AddSecretVersion.

types.name: SecretPayload_Input
types.description: A secret payload resource in the Secret Manager API. This contains the sensitive secret payload that is associated with a SecretVersion.

inputFields.name: data
inputFields.description: The secret data. Must be no larger than 64KiB.

inputFields.name: dataCrc32c
inputFields.description: Optional. If specified, SecretManagerService will verify the integrity of the received data on SecretManagerService.AddSecretVersion calls using the crc32c checksum and store it to include in future SecretManagerService.AccessSecretVersion responses. If a checksum is not provided in the SecretManagerService.AddSecretVersion request, the SecretManagerService will generate and store one for you. The CRC32C value is encoded as a Int64 for compatibility, and can be safely downconverted to uint32 in languages that support this type. https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/design_patterns#integer_types

types.name: Secret_Input
types.description: A Secret is a logical secret whose value and versions can be accessed. A Secret is made up of zero or more SecretVersions that represent the secret data.

inputFields.name: topics
inputFields.description: Optional. A list of up to 10 Pub/Sub topics to which messages are published when control plane operations are called on the secret or its versions.

inputFields.name: expireTime
inputFields.description: Optional. Timestamp in UTC when the Secret is scheduled to expire. This is always provided on output, regardless of what was sent on input.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Output only. The resource name of the Secret in the format `projects/*/secrets/*`.

inputFields.name: ttl
inputFields.description: Input only. The TTL for the Secret.

inputFields.name: tags
inputFields.description: Optional. Input only. Immutable. Mapping of Tag keys/values directly bound to this resource. For example: "123/environment": "production", "123/costCenter": "marketing" Tags are used to organize and group resources. Tags can be used to control policy evaluation for the resource.

inputFields.name: annotations
inputFields.description: Optional. Custom metadata about the secret. Annotations are distinct from various forms of labels. Annotations exist to allow client tools to store their own state information without requiring a database. Annotation keys must be between 1 and 63 characters long, have a UTF-8 encoding of maximum 128 bytes, begin and end with an alphanumeric character ([a-z0-9A-Z]), and may have dashes (-), underscores (_), dots (.), and alphanumerics in between these symbols. The total size of annotation keys and values must be less than 16KiB.

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: The labels assigned to this Secret. Label keys must be between 1 and 63 characters long, have a UTF-8 encoding of maximum 128 bytes, and must conform to the following PCRE regular expression: `\p{Ll}\p{Lo}{0,62}` Label values must be between 0 and 63 characters long, have a UTF-8 encoding of maximum 128 bytes, and must conform to the following PCRE regular expression: `[\p{Ll}\p{Lo}\p{N}_-]{0,63}` No more than 64 labels can be assigned to a given resource.

inputFields.name: etag
inputFields.description: Optional. Etag of the currently stored Secret.

inputFields.name: versionAliases
inputFields.description: Optional. Mapping from version alias to version name. A version alias is a string with a maximum length of 63 characters and can contain uppercase and lowercase letters, numerals, and the hyphen (`-`) and underscore ('_') characters. An alias string must start with a letter and cannot be the string 'latest' or 'NEW'. No more than 50 aliases can be assigned to a given secret. Version-Alias pairs will be viewable via GetSecret and modifiable via UpdateSecret. Access by alias is only be supported on GetSecretVersion and AccessSecretVersion.

inputFields.name: versionDestroyTtl
inputFields.description: Optional. Secret Version TTL after destruction request This is a part of the Delayed secret version destroy feature. For secret with TTL>0, version destruction doesn't happen immediately on calling destroy instead the version goes to a disabled state and destruction happens after the TTL expires.

inputFields.name: createTime
inputFields.description: Output only. The time at which the Secret was created.

types.name: Topic_Input
types.description: A Pub/Sub topic which Secret Manager will publish to when control plane events occur on this secret.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Identifier. The resource name of the Pub/Sub topic that will be published to, in the following format: `projects/*/topics/*`. For publication to succeed, the Secret Manager service agent must have the `pubsub.topic.publish` permission on the topic. The Pub/Sub Publisher role (`roles/pubsub.publisher`) includes this permission.

types.name: Rotation_Input
types.description: The rotation time and period for a Secret. At next_rotation_time, Secret Manager will send a Pub/Sub notification to the topics configured on the Secret. Secret.topics must be set to configure rotation.

inputFields.name: nextRotationTime
inputFields.description: Optional. Timestamp in UTC at which the Secret is scheduled to rotate. Cannot be set to less than 300s (5 min) in the future and at most 3153600000s (100 years). next_rotation_time MUST be set if rotation_period is set.

inputFields.name: rotationPeriod
inputFields.description: Input only. The Duration between rotation notifications. Must be in seconds and at least 3600s (1h) and at most 3153600000s (100 years). If rotation_period is set, next_rotation_time must be set. next_rotation_time will be advanced by this period when the service automatically sends rotation notifications.

types.name: CustomerManagedEncryption_Input
types.description: Configuration for encrypting secret payloads using customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK).

inputFields.name: kmsKeyName
inputFields.description: Required. The resource name of the Cloud KMS CryptoKey used to encrypt secret payloads. For secrets using the UserManaged replication policy type, Cloud KMS CryptoKeys must reside in the same location as the replica location. For secrets using the Automatic replication policy type, Cloud KMS CryptoKeys must reside in `global`. The expected format is `projects/*/locations/*/keyRings/*/cryptoKeys/*`.

types.name: Replication_Input
types.description: A policy that defines the replication and encryption configuration of data.

types.name: UserManaged_Input
types.description: A replication policy that replicates the Secret payload into the locations specified in Replication.UserManaged.replicas

inputFields.name: replicas
inputFields.description: Required. The list of Replicas for this Secret. Cannot be empty.

types.name: Replica_Input
types.description: Represents a Replica for this Secret.

inputFields.name: location
inputFields.description: The canonical IDs of the location to replicate data. For example: `"us-east1"`.

types.name: Automatic_Input
types.description: A replication policy that replicates the Secret payload without any restrictions.

types.name: TestIamPermissionsResponse
types.description: Response message for `TestIamPermissions` method.

fields.name: permissions
fields.description: A subset of `TestPermissionsRequest.permissions` that the caller is allowed.

types.name: TestIamPermissionsRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for `TestIamPermissions` method.

inputFields.name: permissions
inputFields.description: The set of permissions to check for the `resource`. Permissions with wildcards (such as `*` or `storage.*`) are not allowed. For more information see [IAM Overview](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/overview#permissions).

types.name: SetIamPolicyRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for `SetIamPolicy` method.

inputFields.name: updateMask
inputFields.description: OPTIONAL: A FieldMask specifying which fields of the policy to modify. Only the fields in the mask will be modified. If no mask is provided, the following default mask is used: `paths: "bindings, etag"`

types.name: Policy_Input
types.description: An Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy, which specifies access controls for Google Cloud resources. A `Policy` is a collection of `bindings`. A `binding` binds one or more `members`, or principals, to a single `role`. Principals can be user accounts, service accounts, Google groups, and domains (such as G Suite). A `role` is a named list of permissions; each `role` can be an IAM predefined role or a user-created custom role. For some types of Google Cloud resources, a `binding` can also specify a `condition`, which is a logical expression that allows access to a resource only if the expression evaluates to `true`. A condition can add constraints based on attributes of the request, the resource, or both. To learn which resources support conditions in their IAM policies, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/help/conditions/resource-policies). **JSON example:** ``` { "bindings": [ { "role": "roles/resourcemanager.organizationAdmin", "members": [ "user:mike@example.com", "group:admins@example.com", "domain:google.com", "serviceAccount:my-project-id@appspot.gserviceaccount.com" ] }, { "role": "roles/resourcemanager.organizationViewer", "members": [ "user:eve@example.com" ], "condition": { "title": "expirable access", "description": "Does not grant access after Sep 2020", "expression": "request.time < timestamp('2020-10-01T00:00:00.000Z')", } } ], "etag": "BwWWja0YfJA=", "version": 3 } ``` **YAML example:** ``` bindings: - members: - user:mike@example.com - group:admins@example.com - domain:google.com - serviceAccount:my-project-id@appspot.gserviceaccount.com role: roles/resourcemanager.organizationAdmin - members: - user:eve@example.com role: roles/resourcemanager.organizationViewer condition: title: expirable access description: Does not grant access after Sep 2020 expression: request.time < timestamp('2020-10-01T00:00:00.000Z') etag: BwWWja0YfJA= version: 3 ``` For a description of IAM and its features, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/).

inputFields.name: bindings
inputFields.description: Associates a list of `members`, or principals, with a `role`. Optionally, may specify a `condition` that determines how and when the `bindings` are applied. Each of the `bindings` must contain at least one principal. The `bindings` in a `Policy` can refer to up to 1,500 principals; up to 250 of these principals can be Google groups. Each occurrence of a principal counts towards these limits. For example, if the `bindings` grant 50 different roles to `user:alice@example.com`, and not to any other principal, then you can add another 1,450 principals to the `bindings` in the `Policy`.

inputFields.name: auditConfigs
inputFields.description: Specifies cloud audit logging configuration for this policy.

inputFields.name: version
inputFields.description: Specifies the format of the policy. Valid values are `0`, `1`, and `3`. Requests that specify an invalid value are rejected. Any operation that affects conditional role bindings must specify version `3`. This requirement applies to the following operations: * Getting a policy that includes a conditional role binding * Adding a conditional role binding to a policy * Changing a conditional role binding in a policy * Removing any role binding, with or without a condition, from a policy that includes conditions **Important:** If you use IAM Conditions, you must include the `etag` field whenever you call `setIamPolicy`. If you omit this field, then IAM allows you to overwrite a version `3` policy with a version `1` policy, and all of the conditions in the version `3` policy are lost. If a policy does not include any conditions, operations on that policy may specify any valid version or leave the field unset. To learn which resources support conditions in their IAM policies, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/help/conditions/resource-policies).

inputFields.name: etag
inputFields.description: `etag` is used for optimistic concurrency control as a way to help prevent simultaneous updates of a policy from overwriting each other. It is strongly suggested that systems make use of the `etag` in the read-modify-write cycle to perform policy updates in order to avoid race conditions: An `etag` is returned in the response to `getIamPolicy`, and systems are expected to put that etag in the request to `setIamPolicy` to ensure that their change will be applied to the same version of the policy. **Important:** If you use IAM Conditions, you must include the `etag` field whenever you call `setIamPolicy`. If you omit this field, then IAM allows you to overwrite a version `3` policy with a version `1` policy, and all of the conditions in the version `3` policy are lost.

types.name: Binding_Input
types.description: Associates `members`, or principals, with a `role`.

inputFields.name: members
inputFields.description: Specifies the principals requesting access for a Google Cloud resource. `members` can have the following values: * `allUsers`: A special identifier that represents anyone who is on the internet; with or without a Google account. * `allAuthenticatedUsers`: A special identifier that represents anyone who is authenticated with a Google account or a service account. Does not include identities that come from external identity providers (IdPs) through identity federation. * `user:{emailid}`: An email address that represents a specific Google account. For example, `alice@example.com` . * `serviceAccount:{emailid}`: An email address that represents a Google service account. For example, `my-other-app@appspot.gserviceaccount.com`. * `serviceAccount:{projectid}.svc.id.goog[{namespace}/{kubernetes-sa}]`: An identifier for a [Kubernetes service account](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/kubernetes-service-accounts). For example, `my-project.svc.id.goog[my-namespace/my-kubernetes-sa]`. * `group:{emailid}`: An email address that represents a Google group. For example, `admins@example.com`. * `domain:{domain}`: The G Suite domain (primary) that represents all the users of that domain. For example, `google.com` or `example.com`. * `principal://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/subject/{subject_attribute_value}`: A single identity in a workforce identity pool. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/group/{group_id}`: All workforce identities in a group. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/attribute.{attribute_name}/{attribute_value}`: All workforce identities with a specific attribute value. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/*`: All identities in a workforce identity pool. * `principal://iam.googleapis.com/projects/{project_number}/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/{pool_id}/subject/{subject_attribute_value}`: A single identity in a workload identity pool. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/projects/{project_number}/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/{pool_id}/group/{group_id}`: A workload identity pool group. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/projects/{project_number}/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/{pool_id}/attribute.{attribute_name}/{attribute_value}`: All identities in a workload identity pool with a certain attribute. * `principalSet://iam.googleapis.com/projects/{project_number}/locations/global/workloadIdentityPools/{pool_id}/*`: All identities in a workload identity pool. * `deleted:user:{emailid}?uid={uniqueid}`: An email address (plus unique identifier) representing a user that has been recently deleted. For example, `alice@example.com?uid=123456789012345678901`. If the user is recovered, this value reverts to `user:{emailid}` and the recovered user retains the role in the binding. * `deleted:serviceAccount:{emailid}?uid={uniqueid}`: An email address (plus unique identifier) representing a service account that has been recently deleted. For example, `my-other-app@appspot.gserviceaccount.com?uid=123456789012345678901`. If the service account is undeleted, this value reverts to `serviceAccount:{emailid}` and the undeleted service account retains the role in the binding. * `deleted:group:{emailid}?uid={uniqueid}`: An email address (plus unique identifier) representing a Google group that has been recently deleted. For example, `admins@example.com?uid=123456789012345678901`. If the group is recovered, this value reverts to `group:{emailid}` and the recovered group retains the role in the binding. * `deleted:principal://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/{pool_id}/subject/{subject_attribute_value}`: Deleted single identity in a workforce identity pool. For example, `deleted:principal://iam.googleapis.com/locations/global/workforcePools/my-pool-id/subject/my-subject-attribute-value`.

inputFields.name: role
inputFields.description: Role that is assigned to the list of `members`, or principals. For example, `roles/viewer`, `roles/editor`, or `roles/owner`. For an overview of the IAM roles and permissions, see the [IAM documentation](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/roles-overview). For a list of the available pre-defined roles, see [here](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/understanding-roles).

types.name: Expr_Input
types.description: Represents a textual expression in the Common Expression Language (CEL) syntax. CEL is a C-like expression language. The syntax and semantics of CEL are documented at https://github.com/google/cel-spec. Example (Comparison): title: "Summary size limit" description: "Determines if a summary is less than 100 chars" expression: "document.summary.size() < 100" Example (Equality): title: "Requestor is owner" description: "Determines if requestor is the document owner" expression: "document.owner == request.auth.claims.email" Example (Logic): title: "Public documents" description: "Determine whether the document should be publicly visible" expression: "document.type != 'private' && document.type != 'internal'" Example (Data Manipulation): title: "Notification string" description: "Create a notification string with a timestamp." expression: "'New message received at ' + string(document.create_time)" The exact variables and functions that may be referenced within an expression are determined by the service that evaluates it. See the service documentation for additional information.

inputFields.name: description
inputFields.description: Optional. Description of the expression. This is a longer text which describes the expression, e.g. when hovered over it in a UI.

inputFields.name: title
inputFields.description: Optional. Title for the expression, i.e. a short string describing its purpose. This can be used e.g. in UIs which allow to enter the expression.

inputFields.name: location
inputFields.description: Optional. String indicating the location of the expression for error reporting, e.g. a file name and a position in the file.

inputFields.name: expression
inputFields.description: Textual representation of an expression in Common Expression Language syntax.

types.name: AuditConfig_Input
types.description: Specifies the audit configuration for a service. The configuration determines which permission types are logged, and what identities, if any, are exempted from logging. An AuditConfig must have one or more AuditLogConfigs. If there are AuditConfigs for both `allServices` and a specific service, the union of the two AuditConfigs is used for that service: the log_types specified in each AuditConfig are enabled, and the exempted_members in each AuditLogConfig are exempted. Example Policy with multiple AuditConfigs: { "audit_configs": [ { "service": "allServices", "audit_log_configs": [ { "log_type": "DATA_READ", "exempted_members": [ "user:jose@example.com" ] }, { "log_type": "DATA_WRITE" }, { "log_type": "ADMIN_READ" } ] }, { "service": "sampleservice.googleapis.com", "audit_log_configs": [ { "log_type": "DATA_READ" }, { "log_type": "DATA_WRITE", "exempted_members": [ "user:aliya@example.com" ] } ] } ] } For sampleservice, this policy enables DATA_READ, DATA_WRITE and ADMIN_READ logging. It also exempts `jose@example.com` from DATA_READ logging, and `aliya@example.com` from DATA_WRITE logging.

inputFields.name: auditLogConfigs
inputFields.description: The configuration for logging of each type of permission.

inputFields.name: service
inputFields.description: Specifies a service that will be enabled for audit logging. For example, `storage.googleapis.com`, `cloudsql.googleapis.com`. `allServices` is a special value that covers all services.

types.name: AuditLogConfig_Input
types.description: Provides the configuration for logging a type of permissions. Example: { "audit_log_configs": [ { "log_type": "DATA_READ", "exempted_members": [ "user:jose@example.com" ] }, { "log_type": "DATA_WRITE" } ] } This enables 'DATA_READ' and 'DATA_WRITE' logging, while exempting jose@example.com from DATA_READ logging.

inputFields.name: exemptedMembers
inputFields.description: Specifies the identities that do not cause logging for this type of permission. Follows the same format of Binding.members.

types.name: EnableSecretVersionRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for SecretManagerService.EnableSecretVersion.

inputFields.name: etag
inputFields.description: Optional. Etag of the SecretVersion. The request succeeds if it matches the etag of the currently stored secret version object. If the etag is omitted, the request succeeds.

types.name: DestroySecretVersionRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for SecretManagerService.DestroySecretVersion.

inputFields.name: etag
inputFields.description: Optional. Etag of the SecretVersion. The request succeeds if it matches the etag of the currently stored secret version object. If the etag is omitted, the request succeeds.

types.name: DisableSecretVersionRequest_Input
types.description: Request message for SecretManagerService.DisableSecretVersion.

inputFields.name: etag
inputFields.description: Optional. Etag of the SecretVersion. The request succeeds if it matches the etag of the currently stored secret version object. If the etag is omitted, the request succeeds.

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be `null`.
